steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The prior sentence establishes that Wordsworth's retreat to nature was a response to industrialization. The blank sentence then says the poem “bears the imprint” of those changes. The blank must show that bearing the imprint is a logical consequence of being a response to industrialization — not a contrast, addition, or example.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Nevertheless,: Signals contrast or concession. The poem bearing an imprint of industrialization would contradict what came before — but there's no contradiction here; it follows logically. ✗
  • Choice B — For example,: Introduces a specific instance of a general claim. The blank sentence states a conclusion, not an example of one. ✗
  • Choice C — Moreover,: Adds a new point. The blank sentence isn't adding fresh information — it's drawing a conclusion from what was already said. ✗
  • Choice D — As such,: Means “therefore” or “consequently.” Because the poem is a response to industrialization, it therefore bears the imprint of those changes — a direct logical consequence. ✓
D is correct.
